name: Bean-Vegetable Combo
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dry pea (navy) beans (cooked, unsalted, drained)
2 tbsp Bean cooking liquid OR water
recipeInstructions:
- For 2 servings of about 1 cup each, this recipe yields approximately 80 calories per serving with bean cooking liquid and around 180 calories per serving when using water.
1. In a hot skillet or frying pan, heat the margarine over medium heat. Add the onion, carrot, and bay leaf. Stir-fry for about 5 minutes until the vegetables are lightly browned.
2. Add the chopped cabbage to the pan. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Cover the pan and continue cooking over low heat for about 5 minutes, or until the cabbage is tender but still crisp.
3. Add the cooked navy beans (either fresh and drained or canned) along with the bean cooking liquid or water. Stir to combine and cook for an additional 5 minutes at a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
4. Remove the bay leaf before serving.
Note: If using canned navy beans instead of dried, cooked beans, you can omit the salt in step 2. Approximately 196 calories per serving are expected when using bean cooking liquid.
